headline: Rural Broadband Reaches Only 34% of Sabah Households
context: MCMC data shows fixed broadband penetration in rural Sabah reached only 34% by end of 2025 compared to national target of 85%.

cards.big: Only 34% of rural Sabah homes have fixed broadband access
cards.sub: National target is 85% but Sabah trails by 51 percentage points

cards.big: JENDELA programme allocated RM1.2 billion for Sabah connectivity by 2025
cards.sub: Only 680 of 1,450 planned telecommunications towers completed on schedule

cards.big: Average download speed in rural Sabah is 8 Mbps versus 120 Mbps in KL
cards.sub: Students in Kudat and Beluran cannot access online learning platforms

cards.big: Starlink applications from 12,000 Sabah households approved in 2025
cards.sub: Monthly cost of RM220 unaffordable for communities with RM1,200 median income

cards.big: Broadband gaps expose whether digital inclusion reaches beyond urban centres
34% rural broadband in Sabah — digital exclusion compounds every other disadvantage Borneo faces.
